/-!
`dsimp% […] t` runs `dsimp […]` on term `t`.
If `t` is a proof, then it runs `dsimp […]` on the type of `t` instead.

For instance, instead of
```
have foo := ...
dsimp at foo
rw [foo]
```
one can do `rw [dsimp% foo]`.
-/
